reviews.review_title: Concept is great, execution needs work
reviews.review_description: I wanted to like this app. I subscribed for a month, but just could not get into it. When you open the demo before buying/subscribing, it indicates that the quality is reduced, but after subscribing, even the highest quality videos are somewhat blurry and the interface is a bit confusing. There is a persistent text overlay across the bottom of the screen when doing a ride that says something to the effect that "if the video has issues, download it" but if you subscribe to the service, you can't download it so that message is not helpful and is distracting.  I had a lot of freezing and stuttering while trying to stream on a gig connection and times that while my Wahoo was connected, my cycling stats were not counting up. If I did half of a track and came back later, it asked me if I wanted to continue where I left off and when I selected yes, it put me back at the beginning of the track and when I pedaled, the counter moved but the environment did not. There are some smaller environments that are filmed in areas with a lot of people walking in different directions and it causes motion sickness for me and I never get that so be mindful of those tracks.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Kinomap VR on Meta Quest is an absolute game-changer for anyone who wants to make indoor cycling actually exciting. From the moment you put on the headset, you’re transported into stunning real-world routes that feel incredibly immersive — it’s not just exercise, it’s an experience.

Pair it with a cadence sensor and the  synchronization between your movement and the environment is pretty smooth and motivating, making each session feel purposeful and engaging. 

With over 50 locations from around the world to choose from, the realistic visuals, and the interactive features create a perfect blend of fitness and exploration.

If like me, you struggle staying motivated Kinomap VR doesn’t just help you stay active — it makes you want to push further every time. Highly recommended!

Absolutely love this app, thrilled to find 10k and up rides to use on my stationary bike, looking forward to purchasing more rides in these higher resolutions, unfortunately some of the 8k rides are hit and miss with quality, so as mentioned earlier I will wait for the release of further rides 10k upwards.

The app is easy to use and was easy to connect to my bike via the cadence sensor attached to my pedal.

Thank you for creating such an awesome app and making exercise fun, keep those rides coming!!!

First off pick a name
 I spent 15 minutes just trying to find in the meta store. I searched immersion vr, then fit immersion as it's called on the headset only to eventually find the name kinomap vr. Rant over.

The experience is great. How to make it better. 180 vr video. No one is looking behind themselves. I'd rather have half the pov and twice the clarity and smoothness. It's so blurry and choppy looking left and right it induces nausea. It's only tolerable straight ahead and that minimally. Can't read hug roadsigns. Better video and you'll have an amazing app.

This app is beautiful! Even the home menu page is beautifully scenic. I really felt like I was riding a bicycle in these other countries. The views are amazing and so clear! It's absolutely breathtaking. Everyone will enjoy this immersion experience.
Makes riding stationary bike a lot more fun rather than watching TV. Would like to see more of the USA.

I love using this app, and being  able to explore the world while on my exercise bike. I also like that you can earn tokens for every kilometre you travel, so that you can get new routes without paying for them. Another thing I like are the new 10k and 11k  resolution on some routes, which makes all the views sharper and enhances your enjoyment of the rides.
There are cool tracks, but they are visibly compressed

I have been working out in VR for some time now and have moved my workouts to the outside world and bought a stationary bike. Then O fell into this app and thought how amazing it would be 
And now I’m sold.

reviews.review_title: Just what I was searching for
reviews.review_description: This is exactly what I was looking for. I wanted nice scenery for my exercise bike rides. Jus wonderful scenery to watch as I pedal casually. I did not a trainer, or high reps, just a nice environment to ride through.

I've been using this app for the past week with my connected Domyos elliptical. It's fantastic to exercise through the streets of France, on the icy beaches of Iceland, in Japan, Canada... etc. There are days when I do the workout twice because I can't wait until the next day. I love it!!!

reviews.review_title: Just what I have been looking for!
reviews.review_description: I have been trying to find an app like this to use with my Meta quest for years! I can work out on my elliptical and run on the treadmill while watching the videos on Meta quest. I’m excited to work out now! ￼
Great app looks good but having trouble adding credit

This really seems like it has good potential, but it needs some work in terms of usability. I'm trying to use it with my Stages power meter. However, it picked up my Garmin speed sensor instead. I took off the speed sensor and then it used my power meter. It seems like there is no way to configure which sensors it is using. I would also like to use my heart rate sensor, but I'm not sure whether it's picking that up or not. There is a section to determine the equipment, but it seems like that is for setting up with a dedicated indoor bike unit, unlike my bike that is just sitting on the indoor trainer. I probably need to play around with it more and maybe I will be able to update this review.

Looks nice but its no more than a demo ,than you have to buy credits and buy a location

And then you can cycle 90 min a day or buy a pro subscription 49 dollar a month!!!! Dont think so

I have this connected with my Wahoo Kicker. I can’t seem to set the ground level so it doesn’t feel like I’m 6 feet off the ground. Makes me nauseous when I ride with this app. Sad to not have it work better.

I had been dreaming for a long time of an app that would let us travel while riding a stationary bike. There are two other similar apps available on Meta, but they require constant hands movement to move forward, which gets annoying over time. Here, no controllers are needed — tracking is done entirely through the headset, and that’s honestly amazing.

Do we believe in it? Absolutely, 100%!
It really feels like you're strolling through a city, the Alps, a forest, or country road.

Some of the first videos they made can be a bit shaky at times, which can be slightly distracting. But with the latest routes, they’ve managed to improve camera stability and deliver a much smoother experience.

Here are my favorite routes from the ones I’ve tried : 

8 Scenic Routes : Col de Peyresourde, Chishang Rice (Taiwan),   Glencree - Enniskerry (Irlande), Col du Galibier, Alpes d'Huez, Col D'Azet, Around A Pond In The South Of France, Ascent to Puy de Dome

7 Cities Routes : Crossing Sète city, Ride InToronto, Visit Of Budapest, Zengpeng Corner (China),  Nice Monte-Carlo, Monaco-Nice, Paris Sacré-Coeur
Just completed the Moscow ride and felt like I was there. Can't wait to see more.
makes riding stationary bike a lot more fun rather than looking at a wall or tv this places you in a town or country road

​For anyone looking to inject some serious excitement and breathtaking scenery into their indoor cycling routine, this is an absolute game-changer. This cycling routes experience goes far beyond just a static screen, truly immersing you in stunning virtual worlds that make every pedal stroke feel like an adventure.
Great idea and great performance ! Happy to check new roads 🙂

This is a very good app for exercising I purchased two destinations before I made my decision.yes they need to go to work on the app it's not user friendly.and the videos need upgrading and the measurements are in meters but I purchased it just for my exercise experience and that is excellent. Please upgrade your app!!!
Cool enough concept. Had to redownload tracks to get it to work
I've only tried the demo ride so far, but I'm already impressed. This is an amazing addition to my Torneo Comfort B-565BL exercise bike.
Love the App would like some more of Ireland cycling down by one of the beaches would be great
Measurement system - Imperial 👎
I didnt use the full version yet. But I tried the demo version. Seems to be very good. But have to use it fully and see. Any way love the experience I had with the demo version.

Looks interesting . Couldn't  tell much after a demo that tried to show multiple  video clips strung  together  and  cadence  features  not working . I hope  the  non demo  version is better.  I will  update my review after I try a  normal  course and try the cadence  feature

I was looking for an immersive experience whilst using my stationary bike at home. This ticks all the boxes and makes me want to exercise more! Love the variety of tracks on offer and cant wait to explore more!

It was a little to understand what was required to purchase credits but finally got it . My bike is a York fitness and is not on the list so couldn't connect.  It was also hard to find credits. But when I get the hang of it I think it will be good
Great app, I really liked it – everything works perfectly.
I’m looking forward to using this app! I went through the demo and it accurately acquired the information from my stationary bike, and the scenes seem lovely. Can’t wait!
I’m excited to try fit immersion on my stationary bike. Bring able to see new places from my own home adds an exciting new element to exercise. Being able to hear the local radio is an added plus.

I bought credits and then get a message that says daily time allowance.exceeded you can't use the app, you need to subscribe to pro. I don't know what this pro is given there is no explanation, link or possibility or subscribing to it but this is a con. I have bought credit and still can't use it! And the live chat is unavailable.

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry but the daily use (ride) limit is 90 minutes. When using more than this period we consider it is for professional or multi-users use. The pro subscription is 49€/months and gives you access to the entire library availble there: https://fitimmersion.com/pro-subcription/. Edit: there was a bug on daily time use before version 6.32, sorry for that. Now the daily use time is correctly allowing 90 minutes/day.

reviews.review_description: Most of the reviews are to get the free 200 credits , downloaded excitedly to jump on my spin bike only to be disappointed . Just watched an episode of Black Dove on Netflix then instead and had a great spin.

First, very confusing just getting started. 
Second, after figuring out how to actually use the app, it is not that great. I did the demo with a stationary bike and a cadence sensor. 
Third, video was jerky at times. 
Conclusion, maybe this game is a work in progress but as it stands now it's not for me.

I thought could be good to make stationary bike sessions more interesting, the preview tracks were a bit blurry but I thought they would be better when downloaded so I bought some credits. Maybe I picked the wrong track, the 3d Moon, but the resolution isn’t great and it sticks often. Not keen to spend another £8 to be able try another track. The interface isn’t very polished either and a bit frustrating to use. If the tracks were a lot cheaper I’d probably be more forgiving of the other flaws.

Fit Immersion is a nice app. I was able to feel like I was riding my bike in Ireland!
I can't wait to see more of the world from the safety of my stationary bike.

reviews.review_title: Downfall is bad resolution of 360 video
reviews.review_description:
Although I do find it makes my stationary bike sessions more interesting, its flaws mean I probably won’t be using it for long:

* 360 VR video in general is blurry compared to 180 and this is no exception. They should have offered some sharper-resolution 180 videos.

* Despite the bad resolution, the track sizes are huge due to length (over 12 GB). Maybe a trade off has to be made between resolution and size, but again I wouldn’t mind as much if it were sharp 180 video. 

* It’s too confusing figuring out how to redeem earned credits. After finishing a 30-minute ride, it says I earned 5 credits and I can choose an option to send me an email to redeem them (never arrived in inbox or spam). I’d have to do 20 rides to unlock one course, but I don’t see anywhere either in my account on their site or the app where credits are accumulating. 

* HUD graphics can be difficult to view due to being too near the edge of the screen. The UI just seems kind of amateurish in general. 

For now I’ll probably just use the app occasionally, riding my only unlocked course Budapest over and over. I can sort of tolerate the blurriness in short sessions by not looking around too much, but I know my wife would get motion sick from the accentuated choppy/blurry affect that happens when the cyclist makes turns. 

Good concept, not great execution on the Quest platform. 

* Actually buying a course is too expensive, so I wouldn’t, as I’m not getting much value compared to just using VR biking videos on YouTube.

reviews.review_title: Pretty good, has potential to be great.
reviews.review_description:
First off, I don't have the sensors or a smart bike, so I can't speak to that aspect of this app. All I have is a stationary bike. But I really appreciate how the app will just play a fully 360 degree video of a location as I pedal, moving me along at a smooth pace. It does help me feel immersed in the environment, which is far more enjoyable than staring at my wall, or just watching a TV show or something. So kudos to Fit Immersion for that. The video resolution isn't the best, so, while it does feel like I'm there, it also feels like I need glasses or something. A crisp, clean video would be awesome. 

The price for credits seems a little steep. One course (without sound) is 90 credits, which costs just shy of $10USD. There are several courses available, but if you want variety, it's going to cost quite a bit. I'm not likely to buy very many, if any, more courses at that price. 

Finally, the menu in the app is clunky and doesn't have any kind of login option. So you create an account on the Fit Immersion website and buy credits from there, but then there's no login feature in the app to get to your credits. I had to contact customer service via chat on their website to have them add the credits to my app. So that whole process could use some work, as well as the website itself, which doesn't have any links at all to the page where you buy credits. I could only get there by having the app send me an email with a link to it. 

Overall, I think this is a good app for adding some environment and enjoyment to your at-home ride.
video low resolution

Happy to find a VR cycle program that doesn't cause seasickness. Despite a demo mode that pops you in and out of various locales in a short period of time, I did not experience the nausea I did in a similar app. Very small footprint for the program itself, and the cost of adding locations is reasonable in price and storage space.
Lovely app that makes my indoor turbo sessions more bearable through the winter months. The fact that it is compatible with my smart trainer makes it even more emersive.

I have been looking for an immersion app that will allow me to connect with my stationary bike even before purchasing my Quest 3.  This app will keep my mind off that I am exercising. You just enjoy the ride. There are plenty of apps that exercise ones arms and torso,  but not toomany that exercise  the legs. My hats is off to Fit Immersion
The real life experience feel is great.  Makes you workout and enjoy doing it.
Love the views, it makes home exercise seem less work and more fun, and the time goes so much quicker

reviews.review_title: Have a long way to be a mature product
reviews.review_description: The GUI control is not intuitive, e.g. when filling information (height weight etc.), you have to click slider patiently to get to your index, over click, sorry - have to redo; to complete the filling of email, you have to use A button, while other information can use trigger to complete the entry; have to drag the slider to see more tracks (joystick down does not work); once you choose a track, you cannot click on the track to start a demo, but click the 'preview' button. Lastly, I tried to purchase a track at 80C for a test run, and was asked to pay for a minimal of $100 (you can pay more of course).

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, we will improve UI in next version. The minimal cost is $10, not $100 :)

reviews.review_description: The concept is really what I was looking for - a way to turn my boring stationary bike into a VR ride. This app does that, and even is able to track the headset motion to emulate the cadence. I was impressed by that as I could speed up or stop and it was all tracked. The videos (many in France) are interesting. Decent quality to work and appears 3D enough. I wish it were better video so I could read the roadside signs but its just slightly too blurry. The setup screen and HUD could use improvements. But given that the app is free and you only pay for the videos (all less than $10 each) and it works offline - this is reasonable.  If the video resolution were increased and the HUD removable w/ a trigger, it would be amazing.

reviews.review_title: The application I've been waiting for
reviews.review_description: Finally the application I've been waiting for. Immersive videos done right, I can take a ride on my exercise bike visiting fantastic places. It immediately detected my cadence sensor without any problems. Small changes to be made but I have high expectations. Recommended for VR lovers . Thank you guys.

Update: After a few rides I decided to update my review. I really enjoy this app. It connects to my Wahoo sensors, although there is a little lag in the cadence sensor. The google earth tracks are good, but the 3D moon track is awesome! So good it made me a little woozy.  The user interface isn’t the best, but it works. You also earn credits as you ride, so it is more affordable than I originally thought. So far I have not had to shell out any funds to use the app. I highly recommend for economy minded users.


At first glance, I think it will work for my purposes. I like that there is no subscription, but you have to purchase the tracks.
Looks pretty good, wish they were in the US instead of France for contact and support.
I’m a 1st time VR user on a meta3. The demo was amazing! I am excited to try the various rides. The moon one made me feel a little woozy though
Resolution if graphics is not high, but the app is functional and links up well garmin cadence sensor.

reviews.review_title: I try not to leave bad reviews but...
reviews.review_description: This apk doest let you fill your email to buy credits and then if you want to try the Demo, it doesn't work. Videos aren't available to buy, nor they work, except the one in China that I managed to run.... And got stuck 30 seconds after it started. The video that I managed to see (30 seconds) is very low quality,  just terrible. If you want to buy credits you cant so no way to use this. I went to check the reviews, it seems the developer doesn't give a toss, 2 years ago, same problems. Un installing.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback. The tracks in streaming are in lower quality to prevent being stopped every second because of buffering, better test by downloading a free one. You say you got "stuck", was you speed different from 0?

reviews.review_description: This app has great potential to be really good. I’ve tried the other two paid cycling apps, but I wasn’t really a fan of them, so when I saw this app, I was really excited. The idea of cycling and being immersed in real places seemed fun. When I originally downloaded the app, it was extremely glitchy and I could never get the app to recognize my cadence sensor. After some back-and-forth conversations with the developers, they pushed out a new update and now it works a lot more smoother! I would rate the app much higher, but the quality of the video tracks could be a lot better, and although that’s no fault of the developers (360 videos don’t seem to look that great on the Quest), I think using 180 videos, instead of 360, might make the experience much better—at least for the time being. Overall, I’m glad to have an alternative fitness app.

Was looking forward to use this app, but i got dissaponited when i couldn't get it working. It finds my cad sensor and i can only download the demo which doesn't work. Tried to buy credits to download another map which also doesn't  work. I get to the page were it requires my details then it is stop. As the detail page is all in french which i don't understand it seems to complicated to get any use for the app.

developer_response.body: Please try with last version! We had some issues due to Oculus OS updates.

reviews.review_description: Couldn’t find my cadence sensor, couldn’t enter my email to buy credits, demo was inoperative. Complete dud. I know it’s in app lab but come on! I’ll change my rating if the developers get their act together.

developer_response.body: Please check again with latest version! Oculus OS updates caused some issues.

reviews.review_description: I give 5 starts because for the moment is free, but i have some problems, that if dissapears, maybe I will pay for a complete app. First, I cant fix the HUD, all the data is on the edge and I cant see the data clear and doesnt exist an option to move it. Second, I've download the "Under the bridge" circuit and after 8 seconds, the track hangs or freeze. If you close and open it again, start to works but no data (speed, distance,...) I hope they fix it in a future upgrade. I cant do home bike without it :)

I like this app, the only real downside is the size of the downloaded maps.  I got the cadence enabled version which found my sensor straight away.  The movement is very smooth, much better than the subscription cycling app I tried with the added bonus you don't drop through the road or ride through walls :).  The UI and hud needs a little work, the stats move just out of sight for me, and better video quality would be great but I dread to think what that would do to the route filesizes.

reviews.review_title: This is exactly what I was looking for
reviews.review_description: My son and I love it!
